data.content: The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but the generated file is never added to the `Compile` item group. The target needs to include the generated file in the `Compile` items so the compiler picks it up.

Let me also add it to `FileWrites` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me use the skill for this since it's available.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler didn't know about it.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target (after the file is written) with:
- `<Compile Include="..."/>` — tells the compiler to include the generated file
- `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` — ensures `dotnet clean` removes it
